item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Noting that the image appears to be from here, which is copyrighted. As such, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Some questions:

  • Can the person using SCP-1418 be killed while being undead?
  • If the person using SCP-1418 was harmed while being undead, how those wound would heal after the user became alive again?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Same, Acquisitions is one of my favorite tales, and this is pretty fascinating when you think about it.

was she a real lady imprinted on the comb?
an invention of the comb?

Unanswered questions can make or break a skip, and they make this one, I think.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted only because of this item's usage in the tale "Acquisitions"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this one. The item is a little bland, but you've used it with good effect to tell a story, which I appreciate. If I had one criticism, it would be that you maybe spell the story out a little too much. More intuition required by the reader would have made this a tiny bit more interesting, to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was Colonel Mustard, in the lobby, with the antique comb.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Deleted the reference to insect activity.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nuked the memos based on feedback here and on the IRC.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Removed request for test proposals in writing now the memos are gone (thought I thought this was pretty standard), referenced human subjects in Containment Procedures; alteration sentence has already been changed based on SoullessHuman's feedback, edited reference to recovery log to indicate it's the individual assumed to be the previous owner; insect activity would be assumed during non-anomalous putrefaction so need to mention it's absent - will delete if it's considered odd.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Test proposals should be submitted in writing to sector management

Because it has no current researchers? We just kinda left it there?

its anomalous properties become apparent when worn by a human subject

Then why should you care about other "living subjects"?

The subject is rapidly altered, physically and biologically, with tissue samples harvested from subjects during medical exploration indicating this process extends down to a genetic level.

This was mentioned already.

The appearance of the transformed subject closely matches that of the object's previous owner; see Recovery Log 1418.

Uh, does it? Because it says in the Recovery Log you're not even god damn sure if that's the lady. You could've said "It closely matches other people who were turned by it" and give me the exact same information.

Insect activity is absent, but necrophilic bacteria on the body of the subject demonstrate a level of activity commensurate with the most virulent strains of Escherichia coli.

So because E-Coli magically pops up in the body, you need to mention insects don't?

Given the object's properties it cannot even be assumed that Lady ████████ was a single individual rather than several parties utilising SCP-1418

Some surveillance we did. Whoops.

Downboat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks - much of this is stuff that was chucked backwards and forwards on IRC. Reinserted a semicolon for the alteration sentence; 'for all intents and purposes'; biosafety requirements now in procedures; MC&D reference cut back; 'based on the condition of her body'; inquest sentence edited as suggested; internal memos nuked based on feedback I've received thus far.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The subject is rapidly altered, physically and biologically, with tissue samples harvested from subjects during medical exploration indicating this process extends down to a genetic level.

This sentence is really awkward and could be better written perhaps as two separate sentences or at least a semicolon used.

Once transformed the subject is to all intents and purposes biologically dead.

'to all intents and purposes' seems off to me, this may just be me though.

Accordingly, subjects under the influence of SCP-1418 should be considered active biohazards.

This should be in the Containment Procedures, or at least noted somewhere in there.

a high-society concern known to traffick in anomalous objects and which has been classified as a Group of Interest to the Foundation

I don't believe this is particularly necessary, because there's a group of interest page people can check out if they don't know what MC&D is.

she was reported to have been dead for 4 weeks when discovered owing to the condition of her body

I'm confused, do you mean 'she was reported to have been dead for approximately 4 weeks according to the condition of the body'? Or something? I don't understand. Maybe because I'm tired.

Due to Lady ████████ 's high profile and connections to the peerage an official inquest, which failed to record a cause of death, and a public auction of her assets was permitted.

Cutting down the 'which failed to record…' sentence, this sentence confuses me. 'Due to Lady XXXX's high profile and connections to the peerage an official inquest and a public auction of her assets was permitted'. This sentence just makes little sense to me, since the flow (afaik) should be 'Due to X and X, this happened'.

Both internal memos confuse me as to why they need to be there. I don't think they're telling me anything about the article, so why are these internal memos here? Are they supposed to hint an addictive nature or something?

Overall, neutral vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks - added a brief reference in containment procedures for the need for biosafety during tests on living subjects.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
necrophilic

I saw this as "necrophiliac bacteria". Now I feel like a horrible person.

Like the SCP though, and the reference to MC and D that does not feel tacked on (at least to me). Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While the effect is described in an extremely confusing manner, overall I like this, because it doesn't just outright kill someone. Getting transformed, dying, and then getting both of those reversed (in the proper time frame) is refreshing as effects go. Maybe a little about what the subjects remember, or how they feel after having gone through that?

Accordingly, subjects under the influence of SCP-1418 should be considered active biohazards.

Also, this should be in the containment procedures.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, my bad. As I said, it's time to you to make an author page. Let people see your stuff, and rejoice!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! That's right. This is actually my fourth: SCP-1552, SCP-1251, SCP-1447 (the cross-referenced one) and now this :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this is only his second SCP, but seeing how both of his works are good, I'm sure he'll get a page in no time. As for the SCP, it's indeed his.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sregan, do you have an author page? I take it that the cross-referenced SCP is your as well….I'd just like to read others you've read….
Thanks!/
+vote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh, good. I was worried for a second that my mind was stuck in this really weird place and it was just me.

I definitely picked up on the false person/identity theft threads. I just meant that it wouldn't be so useful for that now that Lady Blackbox has a death certificate on file.

I also thought that it could be something that the Lady had commisioned to always appear a certain age, either for vanity's sake or to actually prolong herself against failing health. I really enjoy the scps that give enough little hints at a backstory to set my mind wandering, but stop short of nailing it down to a single answer. Something you've done quite well here, in my opinion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Probably closer to the former than the latter; whether he was using it to work out personal issues or just for recreational purposes is something I didn't really want to get into :) Re the identity theft thing, I've hopefully hinted that this was how it was being used before it was recovered - there's nothing to say that Lady Blank ever actually existed. Unless the comb can be recalibrated with a new person to imitate, the template it's using is hundreds of years old.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is Dr. Rolfus using this thing for some kind of uber-level cross-dressing?
That's the only way I can think of to abuse the properties. One could use it for very effective identity theft, if the Lady wasn't already officially dead that is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wouldn't call them lolfoundation, but I do agree that the memos don't add to this at all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. I also actually like the cross-reference, as long as it's not a glorified self-insert, having an ongoing character is no big issue for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! With the memos I was trying to create some continuity with my last submission, the tulpa, where the good doctor also appears interviewing SCP-1447-1, but I can see that might be a bit close to 'keter duty' for some; will have a think about it. Thanks also for actually adding the SCP number to references in the text :embarassed:

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this one, however I'm not a fan of the memos at the end. To me, it doesn't really add anything to the narrative other than some researchers abuse SCPs. A little too close to lolfoundation for my tastes. Still, the rest is pretty good, so I'm upvoting.
